title: After-Action Report — BaseRuns / Cluster-Luck Decomposition
scope: Follow-up to the SABR analytics suite. Two ships back-to-back: BsR with default coefficients, then the shape refit on top.

rationale.item: "Together they give you a full luck decomposition: BABIP luck (xwOBA), sequencing luck (BsR), and Pythag formula error (k* refit). Three cleanly separated luck dimensions."
MLB-fit `c = (1.4, −0.6, −3.0, 0.1)` underpredicts in O27's ~22 R/G environment by ~55%.

delegation_events.quote: "do we already have BsR? IF NOt, let's add it"

delegation_events.decision: Do the B-coefficient shape refit immediately
delegation_events.quote: "add that now and yeah don't leave it"

actions.description: New o27v2/analytics/base_runs.py — Smyth standard form with HBP (Tango variant), applied to offense and defense (opponent batting); residual actual − predicted is sequencing/cluster luck
League-mean re-center: multiplicative scale on each side so SUM(pred) == SUM(actual); live values B_off ×1.553, B_def ×1.590

Joint coordinate-descent shape refit: _refit_coeffs() minimises team-level SSE jointly across offense and defense (60 datapoints, 4 parameters, warm-started from MLB defaults; 8 outer × 4 coordinates × 50 inner iterations; ~0.4s)
Widened coefficient bounds after c1 hit the 3.0 upper bound (c1 ∈ [0.5, 6.0], c2 ∈ [-3, 1], c3 ∈ [-8, 2], c4 ∈ [-1, 1.5])
Post-fit multiplicative rescale so fitted residuals are mean-zero and SSE figures apples-to-apples
/analytics BsR panel (dual-column, MLB-default vs Refit coeffs, sorted by |Net (refit)|, color cues mirroring the Pythag panel); export build_base_runs_table; route passes base_runs to template

risk_posture: No engine changes. No schema changes. Pure analytics layer on top of existing `game_batter_stats` + `games`.
Reverted before commit; not in tree. (the discarded Pythag-on-BsR-predicted-RS/RA refit)
Default-coefficient pass is now somewhat redundant given the refit pass is strictly better. Kept it because the *gap* between the two columns is itself the headline finding

validation_claims.check_method: Run build_base_runs_table on the demo-season DB and compare SSEs

validation_claims.claim: Sanity checks: SUM(off_luck) ≈ 0 within ±0.2 R; SUM(def_luck) ≈ 0 within ±0.2 R; league_actual_rs ≈ league_actual_ra
validation_claims.check_method: Sum residual columns from the BsR table

validation_claims.claim: corr(e_BsR_net_fit, e_pythag_actual_fitted_kstar) = −0.052 (0.3% shared variance) across 30 teams
validation_claims.check_method: Compute correlation between BsR-net and Pythag-fitted residuals on the live DB

validation_claims.claim: Perturbed the initial point ±20% on each axis and confirmed convergence to within ±0.05 on every coefficient
Empirical correlation between BsR-net and the BsR-input Pythag residual came back at +0.785
validation_claims.check_method: The parallel refit was discarded uncommitted; would need re-implementation to check

non_actions.item: Combined three-bucket luck panel (stacked bar of W% surplus split into BABIP / clustering / leverage with unattributed remainder)

non_actions.reason: It does the opposite ... captures sequencing + leverage combined ... Reverted before commit; not in tree.

non_actions.item: Hard clip on B during ternary search
non_actions.reason: In practice the fit doesn't visit that region from the warm start, but a hard clip on B would be cleaner.
Could add a final Nelder-Mead polish if precision matters more later.

residual_risks: 30 teams × 2 sides = 60 points fitting 4 parameters is 15:1 DoF ... the fitted coefficients will jitter season-to-season. The structural fit (multiple seasons pooled) is the more durable answer.
No B+C > 0 guard during ternary search ... `_bsr` falls back to `D` (HR-only) if `B+C ≤ 0`.
Coordinate descent isn't gradient descent. It can stall in saddle regions if two parameters are highly correlated.
Default-coefficient pass is now somewhat redundant given the refit pass is strictly better.
open_hooks: Single combined panel showing each team's W% surplus split into the three luck buckets as a stacked bar
Closing-thought process pattern: ship the good as a checkpoint, surface the upgrade path, finish the better in the same session
section_headers_original: Corrected math: BsR ↔ Pythag are already orthogonal
unmapped_content: Header metadata: Branch claude/sabr-baseball-analysis-uOD6J; commits e4ba41c (initial BsR), b94c774 (B-coefficient shape refit). | BsR formula: A = H + BB + HBP − HR; B = c1·TB + c2·H + c3·HR + c4·(BB+HBP); C = AB − H; D = HR; BsR = A·B/(B+C) + D. | Demo-season numbers: 162-game season, 30 teams, 56,487 RS = 57,843 RA (~3% gap from playoff exclusions / unfinished games); fitted coefficients table (c1 +1.400→+3.271, c2 −0.600→−0.582, c3 −3.000→−3.858, c4 +0.100→+0.387); intuition — total bases ~2.3× more advancement-valuable in O27, HR double-counts more in a denser environment, walks in front of hits convert. | Top sequencing-luck movers table (PHI −334.5→−235.8 ~30% compression; MIN, OAK, NJC compress; CIN and CHW grow — expected signature of real sequencing variance). | Three-axis luck decomposition table (xwOBA residual = event-level; BsR refit residual = game-level; Pythag k* residual = season-level). | 'Corrected math' section: first draft suggested partial correlation and a wrong fix; Pythag residual is sequencing-free by construction (conditions on RS/RA); recorded so future work doesn't re-walk into it. | Convexity argument: BsR monotone in B, B linear in each c_i, so per-axis SSE map convex; joint problem non-convex but warm start keeps it near a sensible basin.
parser_notes: Includes a self-correcting 'Corrected math' section documenting a wrong first draft and a discarded uncommitted experiment; recorded in unmapped_content with the discarded fix also under non_actions.
